name: A curated list of Microservice Architecture related principles and technologies.
description: A curated list of Microservice Architecture related principles and technologies.

links.title: 1Backend
links.description: AI-native microservices platform.

links.title: Jolie
links.description: Open source microservice-oriented programming language.

links.title: Triton
links.description: Open-source cloud management platform that delivers next generation, container-based, service-oriented infrastructure across one or more data centers.

links.title: Ballerina
links.description: Cloud native programming language.

links.title: Dapr
links.description: Open source runtime for writing highly performant microservices using any programming language.

links.title: Eclipse Microprofile
links.description: An open forum to optimize Enterprise Java for a microservices architecture by innovating across multiple implementations and collaborating on common areas of interest with a goal of standardization.

links.title: Erlang/OTP
links.description: Programming language used to build massively scalable soft real-time systems with requirements on high availability.

links.title: Finagle
links.description: Extensible RPC system for the JVM, used to construct high-concurrency servers.

links.title: Gleam
links.description: A friendly language for building type-safe, scalable systems.

links.title: GraalVM
links.description: High-performance runtime that provides significant improvements in application performance and efficiency which is ideal for microservices.

links.title: Moleculer
links.description: Fast & powerful microservices framework for Node.js, Java, Go and Ruby.

links.title: Open Liberty
links.description: A lightweight open framework for building fast and efficient cloud-native Java microservices.

links.title: Pears
links.description: Peer-to-peer runtime, development and deployment.

links.title: SmallRye
links.description: APIs and implementations tailored for cloud development, including Eclipse MicroProfile.

links.title: ScaleCube
links.description: Toolkit for building reactive microservices for the JVM: low-latency, high-throughput, scalable and resilient.

links.title: Vert.X Toolbox
links.description: A set of Vert.x components to build reactive microservice applications.

links.title: GRPC
links.description: A high performance, open source, general RPC framework that puts mobile and HTTP/2 first. Libraries in C, C++, Java, Go, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Objective-C, PHP and C#.

links.title: Lwan
links.description: High-performance and scalable web server.

links.title: uSockets
links.description: Miniscule cross-platform eventing, networking & crypto for async applications.

links.title: Cap’n Proto RPC
links.description: The Cap’n Proto C++ RPC implementation.

links.title: C++ Micro Services
links.description: An OSGi-like C++ dynamic module system and service registry.

links.title: Enduro/X
links.description: XATMI based service framework for GNU/Linux.

links.title: Pistache
links.description: A high-performance REST toolkit written in C++.

links.title: Poco
links.description: C++ class libraries for building network-based applications and servers.

links.title: Vibe.d
links.description: Asynchronous I/O that doesn’t get in your way, written in D.

links.title: Phoenix
links.description: Framework for building HTML5 apps, API backends and distributed systems.

links.title: Cowboy
links.description: Small, fast, modular HTTP server written in Erlang.

links.title: Echo
links.description: Fast and unfancy HTTP server framework for Go. Up to 10x faster than the rest.

links.title: Go Chassis
links.description: A framework for rapid development of microservices in Go that is easy to integrate with some cloud ecosystems.

links.title: Gorilla
links.description: Web toolkit for the Go programming language.

links.title: Iris
links.description: Fast, simple and efficient micro web framework for Go.

links.title: Scotty
links.description: Micro web framework inspired by Ruby's Sinatra, using WAI and Warp.

links.title: Servant
links.description: Type-level web DSL.

links.title: Yesod
links.description: The Haskell RESTful web framework.

links.title: Compojure
links.description: A concise routing library for Ring/Clojure.

links.title: Duct
links.description: A server-side framework for Clojure.

links.title: System
links.description: Built on top of Stuart Sierra's component library, offers a set of readymade components.

links.title: Tesla
links.description: Common basis for some of Otto.de's Clojure microservices.

links.title: ActiveJ
links.description: Lightweight and fast library for complex high-load distributed applications and Memcached-like solutions.

links.title: Airlift
links.description: Framework for building REST services in Java.

links.title: Armeria
links.description: Open-source asynchronous HTTP/2 RPC/REST client/server library built on top of Java 8, Netty, Thrift and gRPC.

links.title: Disruptor
links.description: High-performance inter-thread messaging library.

links.title: Conjure
links.description: Opinionated set of libraries for defining and creating RESTish/RPC servers and clients based on Feign or Retrofit as a client and Dropwizard/Jersey with JAX-RS service definitions as a server.

links.title: Jersey
links.description: RESTful services in Java. JAX-RS reference implementation.

links.title: Spring Boot
links.description: Makes it easy to create stand-alone, production-grade Spring based applications.

links.title: Http4k
links.description: Lightweight but fully-featured HTTP toolkit written in pure Kotlin that enables the serving and consuming of HTTP services in a functional and consistent way.

links.title: Ktor
links.description: Framework for building asynchronous servers and clients in connected systems using the Kotlin programming language.

links.title: Finatra
links.description: Fast, testable, Scala HTTP services built on Twitter-Server and Finagle.

links.title: Http4s
links.description: A minimal, idiomatic Scala interface for HTTP

links.title: Actionhero
links.description: Multi-transport Node.js API server with integrated cluster capabilities and delayed tasks.

links.title: Express
links.description: Fast, unopinionated, minimalist web framework for Node.js

links.title: Fastify
links.description: Fastify, Fast and low overhead web framework, for Node.js.

links.title: FeathersJS
links.description: An open source REST and realtime API layer for modern applications.

links.title: Hono
links.description: Small, simple, and ultrafast web framework for the Edges. It works on any JavaScript runtime.

links.title: Koa
links.description: Next generation web framework for Node.js

links.title: Loopback
links.description: Node.js framework for creating APIs and easily connecting to backend data sources.

links.title: NestJS
links.description: A Node.js framework for building efficient and scalable server-side applications with a built-in microservices support.

links.title: Cro
links.description: Libraries for creating reactive distributed systems using Perl 6.

links.title: Mojolicious
links.description: Next generation web framework for Perl.

links.title: API Platform
links.description: API-first web framework on top of Symfony with JSON-LD, Schema.org and Hydra support.

links.title: Ecotone
links.description: Framework based on architectural principles of DDD, CQRS and Event Sourcing that provides building blocks to create scalable and extensible applications.

links.title: Hyperf
links.description: Hyperf is an extremely performant and flexible PHP CLI framework based on Swoole 4.5+, powered by the state-of-the-art coroutine server and a large number of battle-tested components.

links.title: Lumen
links.description: Stunningly fast micro-framework.

links.title: Slim
links.description: Micro-framework that helps you quickly write simple yet powerful web applications and APIs.

links.title: Spiral
links.description: Framework designed for long-running applications using [RoadRunner](https://roadrunner.dev/). It offers advanced features like integration with the [Temporal](https://temporal.io/) workflow engine and [Centrifugo](https://centrifugal.dev/) websocket server. It is particularly effective for microservices architecture, providing robust support for REST APIs and gRPC services.

links.title: Swoft
links.description: PHP microservices coroutine framework for building high-performance web systems, APIs, middleware, and basic services.

links.title: Symfony
links.description: Micro-framework based on the Symfony components.

links.title: Bottle
links.description: Fast, simple and lightweight WSGI micro web-framework for Python.

links.title: Connexion
links.description: Swagger/OpenAPI framework for Python on top of Flask with automatic endpoint validation and OAuth2 support.

links.title: Falcon
links.description: Bare-metal Python web API framework for building very fast app backends and microservices.

links.title: FastAPI
links.description: Modern, fast (high-performance), web framework for building APIs with Python 3.6+ based on standard Python type hints.

links.title: Flask
links.description: Python framework for microservices based on Werkzeug and Jinja 2.

links.title: Nameko
links.description: Python framework for building microservices.

links.title: Tornado
links.description: Web framework and asynchronous networking library.

links.title: Twisted
links.description: Event-driven network programming engine.

links.title: Web.py
links.description: Minimalist web framework for Python.

links.title: Grape
links.description: An opinionated framework for creating REST-like APIs

links.title: Hanami
links.description: A modern web framework for Ruby.

links.title: Praxis
links.description: Framework for both designing and implementing APIs.

links.title: Scorched
links.description: Light-weight web framework for Ruby.

links.title: Actix
links.description: Powerful, pragmatic, and extremely fast web framework for Rust.

links.title: Tarpc
links.description: RPC framework for Rust with a focus on ease of use.

links.title: Tokio
links.description: Asynchronous runtime for writing network applications.

links.title: Tower
links.description: Library of modular and reusable components for building robust networking clients and servers.

links.title: Electrode
links.description: Universal React/Node.js application platform.

links.title: Micro Frontends
links.description: Extending the microservice idea to frontend development.

links.title: MiniApp White Paper
links.description: MiniApp standardization white paper.

links.title: Ambassador (c)
links.description: Kubernetes-native API gateway for microservices built on Envoy.

links.title: APIcast
links.description: APIcast is an API gateway built on top of NGINX. It is part of the Red Hat 3scale API Management Platform.

links.title: Caddy
links.description: Extensible HTTP/2 web server with automatic HTTPS.

links.title: Envoy
links.description: Open source edge and service proxy, from the developers at Lyft.

links.title: Istio
links.description: An open platform to connect, manage, and secure microservices.

links.title: Keepalived
links.description: Simple and robust facilities for loadbalancing and high-availability to Linux system and Linux based infrastructures.

links.title: KrakenD
links.description: Open source ultra performance API Gateway.

links.title: Kuma
links.description: Platform agnostic open source control plane for service mesh and microservices.

links.title: Neutrino
links.description: Extensible software load balancer.

links.title: OpenResty
links.description: Fast web application server built on top of Nginx.

links.title: Open Service Mesh
links.description: Lightweight and extensible cloud native service mesh.

links.title: Otoroshi
links.description: Modern HTTP reverse proxy with lightweight API management.

links.title: Spring Cloud Gateway
links.description: API Gateway on top of Spring MVC. Aims to provide a simple, yet effective way to route to APIs.

links.title: Tengine
links.description: A distribution of Nginx with some advanced features.

links.title: Traffic Server
links.description: High-performance building block for cloud services.

links.title: Tyk
links.description: Open source, fast and scalable API gateway, portal and API management platform.

links.title: Central Dogma
links.description: Open-source highly-available version-controlled service configuration repository based on Git, ZooKeeper and HTTP/2.

links.title: Etcd
links.description: Highly-available key-value store for shared configuration and service discovery.

links.title: Eureka
links.description: REST based service that is primarily used in the AWS cloud for locating services for the purpose of load balancing and failover of middle-tier servers.

links.title: Nacos
links.description: Easy-to-use dynamic service discovery, configuration and service management platform.

links.title: SkyDNS
links.description: Distributed service for announcement and discovery of services built on top of etcd. It utilizes DNS queries to discover available services.

links.title: Spring Cloud Config
links.description: Provides server and client-side support for externalized configuration in a distributed system.

links.title: AWS Step Functions (c)
links.description: Coordinate the components of distributed applications and microservices using visual workflows.

links.title: Inngest
links.description: Durable functions for reliable background logic, from background jobs to complex workflows.

links.title: Temporal
links.description: Open source microservices orchestration platform for running mission critical code at any scale.

links.title: Zeebe
links.description: Define, orchestrate, and monitor business processes across microservices.

links.title: Hazelcast
links.description: Open source in-memory data-grid. Allows you to distribute data and computation across servers, clusters and geographies, and to manage very large data sets or high data ingest rates. Mature technology.

links.title: Helix
links.description: Generic cluster management framework used for the automatic management of partitioned, replicated and distributed resources hosted on a cluster of nodes.

links.title: Ignite
links.description: High-performance, integrated and distributed in-memory platform for computing and transacting on large-scale data sets in real-time, orders of magnitude faster than possible with traditional disk-based or flash technologies.

links.title: Libp2p
links.description: A framework and suite of protocols for building peer-to-peer network applications.

links.title: Nomad
links.description: Distributed, highly available, datacenter-aware scheduler.

links.title: Redisson
links.description: Distributed and scalable Java data structures on top of Redis server.

links.title: Serf
links.description: Decentralized solution for cluster membership, failure detection and orchestration.

links.title: Valkey
links.description: A new project to resume development on the formerly open-source Redis project.

links.title: Zenoh
links.description: Pub/sub/query protocol unifying data in motion, data at rest and computations. Efficiently blends traditional pub/sub with geo distributed storage, queries and computations.

links.title: Dkron
links.description: Distributed, fault tolerant job scheduling system.

links.title: Faktory
links.description: Language-agnostic persistent background job server.

links.title: Rundeck (c)
links.description: Job scheduler and runbook automation. Enable self-service access to existing scripts and tools.

links.title: Schedulix
links.description: Open source enterprise job scheduling system lays down ground-breaking standards for the professional automation of IT processes in advanced system environments.

links.title: Kibana
links.description: Flexible analytics and visualization platform.

links.title: LogDNA (c)
links.description: Centralized log management software. Instantly collect, centralize, and analyze logs in real-time from any platform, at any volume.

links.title: ØMQ
links.description: Brokerless intelligent transport layer.

links.title: Beanstalk
links.description: Simple, fast work queue.

links.title: Crossbar
links.description: Open source networking platform for distributed and microservice applications. It implements the open Web Application Messaging Protocol (WAMP).

links.title: Malamute
links.description: ZeroMQ enterprise messaging broker.

links.title: Mosquitto
links.description: Open source message broker that implements the MQTT protocol.

links.title: NATS
links.description: Open source, high-performance, lightweight cloud messaging system.

links.title: RocketMQ
links.description: A low latency, reliable, scalable, easy to use message oriented middleware born from alibaba massive messaging business.

links.title: Beats
links.description: Lightweight shippers for Elasticsearch & Logstash.

links.title: Elastalert
links.description: Easy & flexible alerting for Elasticsearch.

links.title: Ganglia
links.description: A scalable distributed monitoring system for high-performance computing systems such as clusters and grids.

links.title: Grafana
links.description: An open source, feature rich metrics dashboard and graph editor for Graphite, InfluxDB & OpenTSDB.

links.title: Graphite
links.description: Scalable realtime graphing.

links.title: IOpipe (c)
links.description: Application performance monitoring for Amazon Lambda.

links.description: An open source, end-to-end distributed tracing

links.title: OpenTelemetry
links.description: High-quality, ubiquitous, and portable telemetry to enable effective observability.

links.title: Prometheus
links.description: An open source service monitoring system and time series database.

links.title: Riemann
links.description: Monitors distributed systems.

links.title: Sensu
links.description: Monitoring for today's infrastructure.

links.title: SkyWalking
links.description: Application performance monitor tool for distributed systems, especially designed for microservices, cloud native and container-based (Docker, K8s, Mesos) architectures.

links.title: Zabbix
links.description: Open source enterprise-class monitoring solution.

links.title: Reactor.io
links.description: A second-generation Reactive library for building non-blocking applications on the JVM based on the Reactive Streams Specification.

links.title: ReactiveX
links.description: API for asynchronous programming with observable streams. Available for idiomatic Java, Scala, C#, C++, Clojure, JavaScript, Python, Groovy, JRuby, and others.

links.title: RSocket
links.description: Application protocol providing Reactive Streams semantics.

links.title: Raft Consensus
links.description: Consensus algorithm that is designed to be easy to understand. It's equivalent to Paxos in fault-tolerance and performance.

links.title: Svix
links.description: Webhooks service that sends webhooks to your users with full retry schedules, exponential backoff, signature verification, and event types.

links.title: Cerbos Hub

links.title: Dex
links.description: Opinionated auth/directory service with pluggable connectors. OpenID Connect provider and third-party OAuth 2.0 delegation.

links.title: JWT
links.description: JSON Web Tokens are an open, industry standard RFC 7519 method for representing claims securely between two parties.

links.title: OAuth
links.description: Provides specific authorization flows for web applications, desktop applications, mobile phones, and living room devices. Many implementations.

links.title: OpenID Connect
links.description: Libraries, products, and tools implementing current OpenID specifications and related specs.

links.title: Open Ziti
links.description: Zero trust security and overlay networking as pure open source software.

links.title: ORY
links.description: Open source identity infrastructure and services.

links.title: OWASP Agent Memory Guard
links.description: Runtime defense layer for AI agent memory poisoning (OWASP ASI06). Detects tampered memory entries, prompt injection in memory paths, and secret leakage. YAML policies, microsecond latency, zero external dependencies.

links.title: SCIM
links.description: System for Cross-domain Identity Management.

links.title: Vault
links.description: Secures, stores, and tightly controls access to tokens, passwords, certificates, API keys, and other secrets in modern computing.

links.description: Binary serialization library for efficient network communication. For Scala and Scala.js

links.title: Cap’n Proto

links.description: C++11 library for serialization.

links.title: Cheshire
links.description: Clojure JSON and JSON SMILE encoding/decoding.

links.description: Cross-platform, language and transport-independent framework for building and consuming network services.

links.title: Ffjson
links.description: Faster JSON serialization for Go.

links.title: Jackson Afterburner
links.description: Jackson module that uses bytecode generation to further speed up data binding (+30-40% throughput for serialization, deserialization).

links.title: Lite³
links.description: JSON-compatible zero-copy serialization format.

links.title: Druid
links.description: Fast column-oriented distributed data store.

links.description: Open source distributed, scalable, and highly available search server.

links.title: Geode
links.description: Open source, distributed, in-memory database for scale-out applications.

links.title: OpenTSDB

links.title: Pilosa
links.description: Open source, distributed bitmap index that dramatically accelerates queries across multiple, massive data sets.

links.title: Secure Scuttlebutt
links.description: P2P database of message-feeds.

links.title: Mitmproxy

links.title: Mountebank
links.description: Cross-platform, multi-protocol test doubles over the wire.

links.title: Pact

links.title: RestQA
links.description: A tool to manage microservices mocking, unit and performance testing locally with best in class developer experience.

links.title: Specmatic
links.description: Converts API specifications (OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL, gRPC etc) into executable contracts for automated testing, service virtualisation, and backward compatibility validation without writing code.

links.title: Spring Cloud Contract
links.description: TDD to the level of software architecture.

links.title: Wilma
links.description: Combined HTTP/HTTPS service stub and transparent proxy solution.

links.title: AsyncAPI
links.description: AsyncAPI specification, the industry standard for defining asynchronous APIs.

links.title: GraphQL
links.description: Query language designed to build client applications by providing an intuitive and flexible syntax and system for describing their data requirements and interactions.

links.title: JSON:API
links.description: A specification for how a client should request that resources be fetched or modified, and how a server should respond to those requests.

links.title: API Blueprint
links.description: Tools for your whole API lifecycle. Use it to discuss your API with others. Generate documentation automatically. Or a test suite. Or even some code.

links.title: OpenAPI
links.description: The OpenAPI Specification (OAS) provides a consistent means to carry information through each stage of the API lifecycle.

links.title: RAML
links.description: RESTful API Modeling Language, a simple and succinct way of describing practically-RESTful APIs.

links.title: ReDoc
links.description: OpenAPI/Swagger-generated API Documentation.

links.title: Scalar
links.description: Open-source API platform: beautiful API references and 1st-class OpenAPI/Swagger support.

links.title: Slate
links.description: Beautiful static documentation for your API.

links.title: Spring REST Docs
links.description: Document RESTful services by combining hand-written documentation with auto-generated snippets produced with Spring MVC Test.

links.description: Architecture of the World Wide Web, Volume One.

links.description: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax.

links.description: URI Template.

links.description: Registry of protocols built on DIDComm, for high-trust, self-sovereign interactions over any transport.

links.title: IDSA
links.description: The International Data Spaces Association (IDSA) is on a mission to create the future of the global, digital economy with International Data Spaces (IDS), a secure, sovereign system of data sharing in which all participants can realize the full value of their data.

links.description: Grant Negotiation and Authorization Protocol defines a mechanism for delegating authorization to a piece of software, and conveying that delegation to the software. links.title: OIDCONN
links.description: OpenID Connect 1.0 is a simple identity layer on top of the OAuth 2.0 protocol. It allows clients to verify the identity of the end-user based on the authentication performed by an Authorization Server, as well as to obtain basic profile information about the end-user in an interoperable and REST-like manner.

links.title: PASETO
links.description: Paseto is everything you love about JOSE (JWT, JWE, JWS) without any of the many design deficits that plague the JOSE standards. <sup>DRAFT</sup>

links.description: Mechanism for clients to discover a list of named instances of a service, using standard DNS queries.

links.description: A DNS RR for specifying the location of services (DNS SRV).

links.description: Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR).

links.title: Schema.org

links.title: UNIV8
links.description: The Unicode Consortium. The Unicode Standard, Version 8.0.0, (Mountain View, CA: The Unicode Consortium, 2015. ISBN 978-1-936213-10-8).

links.title: RFC3629
links.description: UTF-8, a transformation format of ISO 10646.

links.title: Service per Team
links.description: Each team is responsible for one or more business functions (e.g. business capabilities). A team owns a code base consisting of one or more modules. Its code base is sized so as to not exceed the cognitive capacity of team. The team deploys its code as one or more services. A team should have exactly one service unless there is a proven need to have multiple services.

links.title: Commercetools
links.description: Headless commerce platform.

links.title: Equinox
links.description: Infosys Equinox is a human-centric commerce and marketing platform that supports rich, hyper-personalized experiences across any channel and touchpoint.

links.title: Flamingo
links.description: Framework to build flexible and modern e-commerce applications.

links.title: Autonomy, Hyperconnectivity, and Residual Causality
links.description: Philosophical introduction to the design of adaptive hyperliminal systems through complexity science theories.

links.title: AKF Scale Cube
links.description: Model depicting the dimensions to scale a service.

links.title: Canary Release
links.description: Technique to reduce the risk of introducing a new software version in production by slowly rolling out the change to a small subset of users before rolling it out to the entire infrastructure and making it available to everybody.

links.title: CAP Theorem
links.description: States that it is impossible for a distributed computer system to simultaneously provide all three of the following guarantees: Consistency, Availability and Partition tolerance.

links.title: Microservice Architecture
links.description: Particular way of designing software applications as suites of independently deployable services.

links.title: Microservices – Please, don’t
links.description: Critical advice about some problems regarding a microservices approach.

links.title: Microservices Trade-Offs
links.description: Guide to ponder costs and benefits of the mircoservices architectural style.

links.title: Reactive Manifesto
links.description: Reactive systems definition.

links.title: Reactive Streams
links.description: Initiative to provide a standard for asynchronous stream processing with non-blocking back pressure.

links.title: Testing Strategies in a Microservice Architecture
links.description: Approaches for managing the additional testing complexity of multiple independently deployable components.

links.title: Cloud Native Computing Foundation
links.description: The Cloud Native Computing Foundation builds sustainable ecosystems and fosters a community around a constellation of high-quality projects that orchestrate containers as part of a microservices architecture.

links.title: Microservice Patterns
links.description: Microservice architecture patterns and best practices.

links.title: Microservice Antipatterns and Pitfalls
links.description: Microservice mostly known antipatterns and pitfalls.
